Container.Name: A1111-WebUI-Aegisnir
Container.Description:
AUTOMATIC1111 Stable Diffusion WebUI for Unraid.
This template is configured for a locally built test image: a1111-webui-aegisnir:local Build this image on your Unraid host before creating the container.
README: https://github.com/aegisnir/A1111-unraid/blob/main/README.md
## Security Hardening Defaults The template now sets the following security options by default: - --read-only: Makes the container filesystem read-only except for explicitly mounted paths (like /data). - --tmpfs /tmp:rw,noexec,nosuid,size=2g: Provides a safe, writable /tmp. - --security-opt no-new-privileges:true: Prevents processes from gaining new privileges. - --cap-drop=ALL: Drops all Linux capabilities except those required by the base image. - --pids-limit=2048: A practical default to contain runaway process spawning without being overly restrictive.
To change PID limit in Unraid: edit this container, switch to advanced view, adjust --pids-limit=<value> in Extra Parameters, and apply.
DATA DIRECTORY WARNING: The path used with --data-dir grows very large very quickly because it can hold models, outputs, extensions, caches, and other working data. I recommend storing it outside of appdata when possible. You can still place it inside appdata if you want the convenience, but it may consume space very quickly and can fill the location you use for Docker-related storage.
The default host path is set to /mnt/user/ai/data/ so the large working set lives outside appdata by default.
Self-healing data directory: The container automatically corrects /data ownership on startup if Docker recreated the directory as root (for example, after deleting the host data path). In most cases no user action is needed. A log message will confirm when ownership was corrected. If the auto-fix fails (NFS root squash, unusual host permissions), the log will explain the manual fix.
Authentication defaults: This container enables the AUTOMATIC1111 login page by default unless you explicitly override auth flags in COMMANDLINE_ARGS. Default WebUI username: admin Default WebUI password placeholder: changeme-now The container will refuse to start until you replace that password or explicitly manage auth via COMMANDLINE_ARGS. For live deployments, prefer WEBUI_AUTH_FILE over WEBUI_PASSWORD. WEBUI_PASSWORD is intended for convenience/testing and credentials may appear in logs in some startup paths. You can mount an auth file and set WEBUI_AUTH_FILE to avoid storing credentials directly in template variables.
Model checkpoint note: The default args include --no-download-sd-model. If no checkpoint exists yet, startup can report "No checkpoints found". Add a .safetensors or .ckpt file under /data/models/Stable-diffusion.
Container.Overview:
This template is configured for a local test image by default. If you maintain your own custom image, you can override the Repository field. The default Extra Parameters include --runtime=nvidia so Docker can expose the NVIDIA runtime inside the container. Full setup and usage docs: https://github.com/aegisnir/A1111-unraid/blob/main/README.md
This template passes Automatic1111 data storage through --data-dir.
- Models, outputs, extensions, caches, and other working data are stored in the mapped data share instead of the container filesystem. - This allows you to preserve your models and outputs across container updates and manage them from the host more easily. - The data directory can grow very large very quickly, so I recommend storing it outside of appdata when possible. - Appdata works if you prefer it, but be aware of how quickly it can fill your Docker storage area. - If startup fails with a Torch GPU check error, you can temporarily add --skip-torch-cuda-test to COMMANDLINE_ARGS for troubleshooting. - If first-start dependency installation fails, remove /data/venv and retry after rebuilding the image so the bootstrap can recreate a clean environment. - WebUI login is enabled by default unless you explicitly set your own --gradio-auth or --gradio-auth-path in COMMANDLINE_ARGS. - If you delete the host data directory and Docker recreates it as root, the container self-heals ownership automatically on next start.

Config.Name: WebUI
Config.Description: Port exposed by the AUTOMATIC1111 WebUI (default: 7860).

Config.Name: Data Directory
Config.Description: Host path mapped to --data-dir. Default: /mnt/user/ai/data/ (outside appdata).

Config.Name: WebUI Username
Config.Description: Default username for the AUTOMATIC1111 login page. Change this from the default before exposing the container to other users.

Config.Name: WebUI Password
Config.Description: Password for the AUTOMATIC1111 login page. The container will refuse to start until you replace the default placeholder value. Use WEBUI_PASSWORD for convenience/testing; for live deployments prefer WEBUI_AUTH_FILE. Credentials may appear in logs in some startup paths.

Config.Name: WebUI Auth File
Config.Description: Recommended for live deployments. Path inside the container to an AUTOMATIC1111 auth file. If set, this takes precedence over WEBUI_USERNAME/WEBUI_PASSWORD unless you explicitly pass --gradio-auth or --gradio-auth-path in COMMANDLINE_ARGS.

Config.Name: API Auth Mode
Config.Description: Controls API authentication. 'mirror-webui' reuses WEBUI_USERNAME/WEBUI_PASSWORD for --api-auth. 'disabled' leaves API auth unset unless you pass your own --api-auth in COMMANDLINE_ARGS. Avoid sharing logs that may include custom auth arguments.

Config.Name: API Auth File Mode
Config.Description: Controls API authentication when WEBUI_AUTH_FILE is set. 'mirror-webui-file' mirrors the auth file into --api-auth. 'disabled' leaves API auth unset unless you pass your own --api-auth in COMMANDLINE_ARGS.

Config.Name: UMASK
Config.Description: Optional file creation mask (octal), for example 0027. Leave empty to keep the container default behavior and easier host-side access.

Config.Name: Extra Args
Config.Description: Extra arguments for launch.py. Default includes --data-dir /data, --xformers, and --no-download-sd-model so the container does not silently download a large default checkpoint. Provide your own model under /data/models/Stable-diffusion or override this if you intentionally want automatic download behavior. For GPU errors, try adding --skip-torch-cuda-test temporarily.
